rationale: Y's review closely reconstructs the ground-truth reviewer's actual reasoning (already_submitted is safe to drop because both submit_initial/submit_update reject list prompts identically), backing it with concrete stage_pool.py:951/1022 line citations that match the human comment's logic almost exactly. X touches the same area but its follow-up comment ('remove already_submitted as dead code') actually contradicts its own earlier validated note that the variable is used elsewhere, and it introduces unverifiable specifics (DiffusionOutput having .finished/.request_id fields, a fabricated issue #4839 reference) that hurt precision. Both correctly avoid inventing a blocking bug, but Y reaches the same conservative APPROVE verdict as the real reviewers with tighter, better-grounded justification, while X pads with speculative test/docstring asks of uncertain validity.
